title: IT & Software
description: Essential vocabulary for software development and IT professionals

video.title: Software Development English for IT Professionals

vocabulary.meaning: A step-by-step procedure for solving a problem
vocabulary.example: The sorting algorithm runs in O(n log n) time.

vocabulary.meaning: The process of finding and fixing errors in code
vocabulary.example: She spent hours debugging the authentication module.

vocabulary.meaning: Releasing software to a live environment
vocabulary.example: The deployment to production went smoothly.

vocabulary.meaning: A storage location for code managed by version control
vocabulary.example: Please clone the repository before making changes.

vocabulary.meaning: A request to merge code changes into a branch
vocabulary.example: Submit a pull request when your feature is ready.

vocabulary.meaning: A reusable set of libraries for building applications
vocabulary.example: We use React as our front-end framework.

vocabulary.meaning: Application Programming Interface — a way for programs to communicate
vocabulary.translation: giao diện lập trình ứng dụng
vocabulary.example: The mobile app calls the REST API to fetch data.

vocabulary.meaning: An organized collection of structured data
vocabulary.example: We migrated from MySQL to PostgreSQL database.

vocabulary.meaning: A computer that provides services to other computers
vocabulary.example: The server handles thousands of requests per second.

vocabulary.meaning: Delivering computing services over the internet
vocabulary.example: We moved our infrastructure to cloud computing.

vocabulary.meaning: A software development methodology using iterative cycles
vocabulary.example: Our team follows agile development with two-week sprints.

vocabulary.meaning: A fixed time period to complete a set of tasks in agile
vocabulary.example: We plan our tasks at the beginning of each sprint.

vocabulary.meaning: Restructuring code without changing its behavior
vocabulary.example: Refactoring improved the codebase maintainability.

vocabulary.meaning: The ability of a system to handle increased load
vocabulary.example: Scalability is a key requirement for the new platform.

vocabulary.meaning: The delay between a request and response in a system
vocabulary.example: We need to reduce API latency below 100 milliseconds.

vocabulary.meaning: Converting data into a coded form to prevent unauthorized access
vocabulary.example: All passwords are stored with AES encryption.

vocabulary.meaning: Verifying the identity of a user or system
vocabulary.example: Two-factor authentication adds an extra security layer.

vocabulary.meaning: An architecture style splitting apps into small independent services
vocabulary.example: We broke the monolith into microservices last year.

vocabulary.meaning: A system that tracks changes to files over time
vocabulary.example: Git is the most popular version control system.

vocabulary.meaning: Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery — automated build and release
vocabulary.translation: tích hợp và phân phối liên tục
vocabulary.example: Our CI/CD pipeline runs tests on every commit.

vocabulary.meaning: Packaging software with its dependencies into containers
vocabulary.example: Docker enables containerization of our services.

vocabulary.meaning: A device that distributes network traffic across servers
vocabulary.example: The load balancer routes requests to healthy servers.

vocabulary.meaning: Software with publicly available source code
vocabulary.example: Linux is a famous open source operating system.

vocabulary.meaning: Software that connects different applications or services
vocabulary.example: The middleware handles authentication between services.

vocabulary.meaning: The maximum data transfer rate of a network connection
vocabulary.example: Video streaming requires high bandwidth.

vocabulary.meaning: Temporary storage for frequently accessed data
vocabulary.example: We use Redis as a cache layer to speed up queries.

vocabulary.meaning: A function that calls itself to solve sub-problems
vocabulary.example: Tree traversal is often implemented with recursion.

vocabulary.meaning: A contract defining how components interact
vocabulary.example: All payment providers implement the same interface.

vocabulary.meaning: A class deriving properties and methods from a parent class
vocabulary.example: Inheritance reduces code duplication.

vocabulary.meaning: The ability of objects to take many forms
vocabulary.example: Polymorphism allows flexible and extensible code design.

vocabulary.meaning: Providing dependencies to a class from outside
vocabulary.example: Dependency injection makes unit testing much easier.

vocabulary.meaning: Testing individual components of code in isolation
vocabulary.example: Every function should have at least one unit test.

vocabulary.meaning: Testing how multiple components work together
vocabulary.example: Integration tests verify the API endpoint responses.

vocabulary.meaning: The cost of shortcuts taken during development
vocabulary.example: We need to pay down our technical debt this quarter.

vocabulary.meaning: A prioritized list of features and tasks
vocabulary.example: The product backlog has 50 items to implement.

vocabulary.meaning: A person with interest or investment in a project
vocabulary.example: All stakeholders approved the new feature roadmap.

vocabulary.meaning: An isolated test environment safe for experimentation
vocabulary.example: Test the payment flow in the sandbox environment first.

vocabulary.meaning: A specific URL where an API receives requests
vocabulary.example: The /users endpoint returns all registered users.

vocabulary.meaning: A request for data from a database
vocabulary.example: This SQL query retrieves orders from the last 30 days.

vocabulary.meaning: A string used for authentication or session management
vocabulary.example: The JWT token expires after 24 hours.

vocabulary.meaning: An automated message sent from one app to another on an event
vocabulary.example: The payment gateway sends a webhook on successful charge.

vocabulary.meaning: A bug reintroduced after previously being fixed
vocabulary.example: The update caused a regression in the login flow.

vocabulary.meaning: An early sample or model built to test a concept
vocabulary.example: We built a prototype in two days to validate the idea.

vocabulary.meaning: Written descriptions of how software works
vocabulary.example: Good documentation helps new developers onboard faster.

vocabulary.meaning: Systematic examination of source code by peers
vocabulary.example: All changes require code review before merging.

vocabulary.meaning: An urgent patch applied to fix a critical bug in production
vocabulary.example: We deployed a hotfix to fix the login crash.

vocabulary.meaning: The time a system has been running without failure
vocabulary.example: We guarantee 99.9% uptime in our SLA.

vocabulary.meaning: The amount of data processed by a system per unit time
vocabulary.example: The new queue system doubled our throughput.

vocabulary.meaning: Standard reusable code with little customization needed
vocabulary.example: This template reduces the boilerplate for new projects.

vocabulary.meaning: The rules that define the structure of a programming language
vocabulary.example: Python syntax is clean and easy to read.

vocabulary.meaning: The period when a program is executing
vocabulary.example: A runtime error crashed the application in production.

quiz.question: What is an algorithm?
options: A step-by-step procedure for solving a problem

quiz.question: What is a pull request?
options: A request to merge code changes into a branch

quiz.question: What is debugging?
options: Finding and fixing errors in code

quiz.question: What is a cache?
options: Temporary storage for frequently accessed data

quiz.question: What does 'scalability' mean in IT?
options: The ability to write clean code
The ability of a system to handle increased load

quiz.question: What is a sprint in agile development?
options: A fixed time period to complete tasks

quiz.question: What is technical debt?
options: The cost of shortcuts taken during development

quiz.question: What is a webhook?
options: A type of fish hook in programming
An automated message sent between apps on an event
